message:
feat(abi): port 9 _nss_dns_* NSS DNS plugin lookup stubs (bd-2poiz)

Pair with the nss_files set/get/end stubs already shipped. Each
returns NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND (= 0) and sets *errnop = ENOENT +
*h_errnop = HOST_NOT_FOUND (= 1) when non-NULL, so the NSS dispatch
layer falls through to the next module. Without a real resolver-
backed NSS_DNS module we never claim to have an answer.

  - _nss_dns_getcanonname_r(name, *buf, buflen, **result, *errnop,
    *h_errnop)
  - _nss_dns_gethostbyname_r(name, *result, *buf, buflen, *errnop,
    *h_errnop)
  - _nss_dns_gethostbyname2_r(name, af, ...)
  - _nss_dns_gethostbyname3_r(name, af, ..., *ttlp, **canonp)
  - _nss_dns_gethostbyname4_r(name, **pat, *buf, buflen, *errnop,
    *h_errnop, *ttlp)
  - _nss_dns_gethostbyaddr_r(addr, len, af, ...)
  - _nss_dns_gethostbyaddr2_r(addr, len, af, ..., *ttlp)
  - _nss_dns_getnetbyname_r(name, ...)
  - _nss_dns_getnetbyaddr_r(net, af, ...)

A new `nss_set_h_errnop_host_not_found` helper centralizes the
h_errno write to keep each stub body trivial.

Tests: 4 abi tests covering getcanonname, the 4-entry gethostbyname
family, the 2-entry gethostbyaddr family, and the 2-entry getnetby
family — each verifies NOTFOUND return + errnop=ENOENT + h_errnop=
HOST_NOT_FOUND through both NULL and non-NULL output slots.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2o7i","title":"Fix pthread child-thread shared-memory test hang","description":"Workspace test gate stalls in frankenlibc-core on the pthread thread shared-memory unit case. Reproduces as a single-test run and blocks closure work. Need root-cause and deterministic fix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"WindyKite","created_at":"2026-02-18T19:56:12.313379972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T21:49:36.176713481Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T21:49:36.176686841Z","close_reason":"Fixed create_thread TID republish race causing join hang; targeted reproducer passes via rch. Workspace gate run completed but failed on unrelated stdio_abi printf_redirection test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pthread","testing","triage"],"comments":[{"id":773,"issue_id":"bd-2o7i","author":"WindyKite","text":"Root cause + patch: create_thread was writing handle.tid in parent after clone return. That late store can race with CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID when child exits quickly and resurrect stale non-zero TID, causing join_thread to wait forever. Patched crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs to remove the parent-side tid.store and rely on CLONE_PARENT_SETTID publication. Validation via rch: cargo fmt --check PASS; cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS; targeted reproducer cargo test -p frankenlibc-core child_thread_can_write_shared_memory -- --nocapture --test-threads=1 PASS repeatedly on remote worker. Full cargo test --workspace --all-targets was attempted via rch but rch daemon instability/queue contention produced duplicated long-running jobs (b-6482/b-6494), so full-suite completion evidence is pending queue recovery.","created_at":"2026-02-18T21:48:09Z"}]}

+{"id":"bd-2poiz","title":"PORT: 9 _nss_dns_* NSS plugin lookup stubs","description":"Add the 9 _nss_dns_* NSS DNS plugin lookup stubs. Pair with the nss_files set/get/end stubs already shipped. Each returns NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND (= 0) and sets *errnop = ENOENT + *h_errnop = HOST_NOT_FOUND (= 1) when non-NULL, so the NSS dispatch layer falls through to the next module. Functions: _nss_dns_getcanonname_r, _nss_dns_gethostbyname_r, _nss_dns_gethostbyname2_r, _nss_dns_gethostbyname3_r, _nss_dns_gethostbyname4_r, _nss_dns_gethostbyaddr_r, _nss_dns_gethostbyaddr2_r, _nss_dns_getnetbyname_r, _nss_dns_getnetbyaddr_r. Signatures match the canonical glibc nss_dns convention so register placement of errnop / h_errnop matches what callers actually pass.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:49:43.779296239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:49:43.779296239Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2pw","title":"Kernel: SOS barrier certificates (design + offline artifact format)","description":"Design SOS-derived barrier certificates for runtime admissibility.

Background:
- Barrier certificates give formal invariance: once inside safe set, system stays inside under allowed actions.
- We want heavy SOS/SDP work offline; runtime does cheap polynomial evaluation.

Design tasks:
- Choose 1-2 concrete invariants to start (allocator/quarantine depth, pointer provenance, bootstrap ordering).
- Define state vector x (small, observable at runtime) and safe set S.
- Define polynomial barrier B(x) and rule: allow if B(x) >= 0, deny/repair if B(x) < 0.
- Define artifact format: coefficients + scaling + hash + proof metadata.

Acceptance criteria:
- Clear mapping from invariants to barrier inputs.
- Artifact is versioned and deterministic; runtime verifier is trivial.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleOwl","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:26.210389959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:24:37.625758402Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:24:37.625728966Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":775,"issue_id":"bd-2pw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claimed by GentleOwl. Starting design of SOS-derived barrier certificates for runtime admissibility. Will investigate existing barrier.rs, runtime_math decision flow, and identify concrete invariants for allocator/quarantine depth and pointer provenance.","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:20:59Z"},{"id":776,"issue_id":"bd-2pw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design Complete (GentleOwl)

Design document: crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/sos_barrier_design.md

### Summary of deliverables:

**Two concrete invariants:**

1. **Invariant A: Quarantine Depth Safety Envelope** (legacy anchor: malloc/nptl)
   - State: (depth, contention, adverse_rate, lambda_latency) — 4 vars, normalized [0,1]
   - Degree-3 polynomial barrier B_A(x) with DSOS/SDSOS relaxation
   - Safe set: depth provides sufficient UAF detection within latency budget
   - Cadence-gated (every 256 frees), ~100ns evaluation
   
2. **Invariant B: Pointer Provenance Admissibility** (legacy anchor: elf/dl-*/malloc arena)
   - State: (risk_ppm, validation_depth, bloom_fp_rate, arena_pressure) — 4 vars
   - Degree-2 polynomial in fixed-point ppm arithmetic (NO floats on hot path)
   - Safe set: validation depth adequate for current risk level
   - Hot-path (every decide()), <15ns evaluation

**Artifact format:**
- BarrierCertificate struct: version, name, legacy_anchor, state_indices, num_variables, degree, monomial_exponents, gram_upper, normalization, threshold, proof_hash, evaluation_cadence
- Static const data compiled into binary (no file I/O)
- SHA-256 proof hash verified at init, fallback to predicate barrier on mismatch
- Versioned, deterministic, immutable

**Key constraints met:**
- Clear mapping from invariants to barrier inputs ✓
- Artifact is versioned and deterministic ✓
- Runtime verifier is trivial (quadratic form or expanded polynomial) ✓
- Hot-path budget: <15ns for Invariant B, <100ns cadenced for Invariant A ✓
- Monotone escalation only (barrier violation cannot de-escalate) ✓","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:24:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2r0","title":"Perf budget CI: enforce strict<20ns and hardened<200ns targets on selected hot APIs","description":"Extreme optimization mapping + AGENTS constraints.

Deliverables:
- Add CI checks for hot-path latency budgets by mode.
- Include variance guardrails and repeat-runs policy.

Acceptance:
- Budget breach fails CI with hotspot attribution report.
- Budget exceptions require explicit temporary waiver bead.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): perf_budget_test (8 pass) + gate script. All budget tiers defined, variance guardrails reasonable, waivers validated, hotpath symbols match matrix.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:12.266579047Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z","close_reason":"Perf budget CI enforcement operational. 8 harness tests pass. check_perf_budget.sh validates budget tiers, variance guardrails, waivers, and hotpath symbol classification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","critique","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2r0","depends_on_id":"bd-2bd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2r0","depends_on_id":"bd-2wp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2r0","depends_on_id":"bd-4rl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2r0","depends_on_id":"bd-f7r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

I read AGENTS.md + README.md end-to-end and did an architecture investigation (ABI→membrane→core + harness/scripts). MCP Agent Mail tool calls are currently timing out in this environment, so I will coordinate via bead comments for now. I just claimed bd-33p.1 (canonical evidence schema v2) and will push updates there; ping me here if you need anything or see conflicts.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:12:48Z"},{"id":778,"issue_id":"bd-2ry","author":"RusticCoast","text":"Consolidated closure evidence (RusticCoast):

All bd-2ry dependency subtasks are closed and integrated:
- bd-2xp3: clean-room semantics contract + scope ledger
- bd-ahjd: deterministic nested/edge fixture pack + triage docs
- bd-146t: core phase-1 capture/restore invariant guards + deterministic logs
- bd-24b6: ABI wiring with strict/hardened mode-aware validation + explicit deferred transfer signaling

Implemented strategy outcome for this wave:
1) Behavior is explicit and constrained (not silent):
- capture/validation wired in core+ABI with deterministic guards
- true non-local stack transfer backend remains explicitly deferred and signaled
2) signal-mask interaction handling is explicit in ABI metadata path and fixture/evidence logs
3) no silent host call-through in setjmp ABI path
4) support matrix remains constrained (setjmp-family not advertised as production surface), consistent with deferred transfer backend posture

Deterministic gates/tests passing:
- scripts/check_setjmp_semantics_contract.sh
- scripts/check_setjmp_fixture_pack.sh
- scripts/check_setjmp_phase1_core.sh
- scripts/check_setjmp_abi_wiring.sh
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test setjmp_semantics_contract_test -- --nocapture
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test setjmp_fixture_pack_test -- --nocapture
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test setjmp_phase1_core_test -- --nocapture
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test setjmp_abi_wiring_test -- --nocapture
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi setjmp_abi::tests -- --nocapture
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core phase1_ -- --nocapture

Primary artifact bundles:
- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-2xp3/*
- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-ahjd/*
- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-146t/*
- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-24b6/*

Result: bd-2ry deliverables for strategy + constrained semantics + tested behavior are satisfied and evidence-indexed.","created_at":"2026-02-14T08:36:25Z"}]}
@@ -315,7 +316,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2vv.7","title":"Symbol expansion: signal family with safety","description":"Complete signal family coverage with async-signal-safety.

Current State:
- signal, sigaction: likely partial
- sigprocmask: likely RawSyscall
- raise, kill: likely RawSyscall
- sigsetjmp/siglongjmp: requires setjmp (bd-2ry)

Target Functions:
Basic signal handling:
- signal, sigaction
- sigprocmask, pthread_sigmask
- sigemptyset, sigfillset, sigaddset, sigdelset, sigismember

Signal delivery:
- raise, kill, killpg
- sigqueue (with value)

Signal waiting:
- pause, sigsuspend, sigwait, sigwaitinfo, sigtimedwait

Stack:
- sigaltstack

Non-local jump:
- sigsetjmp, siglongjmp (depends on setjmp impl)

Implementation Notes:
Signal handling is security-critical:
- Async-signal-safety requirements (can't call malloc in handler)
- Signal frame layout is architecture-specific
- SA_SIGINFO vs traditional handler signatures

TSM Considerations:
- Signal handlers run in restricted context
- Membrane must be async-signal-safe
- Validation in signal context must be fast/simple

Testing:
- Unit: signal mask operations
- Integration: signal handler registration and delivery
- Concurrency: signals + threads interaction
- E2E: Ctrl-C handling, SIGTERM graceful shutdown

Success Criteria:
- All signal.h functions classified
- Signal handlers work correctly
- No deadlocks in signal + membrane interaction

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:00.359003874Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T21:36:53.952822168Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T21:36:53.952752548Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","signal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.7","depends_on_id":"bd-25n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":898,"issue_id":"bd-2vv.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Signal family expansion complete: added 11 new ABI functions (killpg, sigprocmask, pthread_sigmask, sigemptyset, sigfillset, sigaddset, sigdelset, sigismember, pause, sigsuspend, sigaltstack). Version script updated with all 11 symbols. Support matrix updated (286→297 total). Reality report, README, and FEATURE_PARITY docs all updated. All quality gates pass: cargo check, clippy, fmt, signal tests (17/17), drift test.","created_at":"2026-02-17T21:36:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vv.8","title":"Symbol expansion: process family","description":"Complete process family coverage.

Current State:
- fork, execve: likely RawSyscall
- wait, waitpid: likely RawSyscall
- getpid, getppid, getuid, etc.: RawSyscall
- system, popen: likely stubs

Target Functions:
Process creation:
- fork, vfork (deprecated but used)
- execve, execl, execle, execlp, execv, execvp, execvpe
- posix_spawn, posix_spawnp

Process waiting:
- wait, waitpid, wait3, wait4
- waitid

Process IDs:
- getpid, getppid, getpgid, getsid
- setpgid, setsid

User/Group IDs:
- getuid, geteuid, getgid, getegid
- setuid, seteuid, setreuid, setresuid
- setgid, setegid, setregid, setresgid
- getgroups, setgroups

High-level:
- system (fork + exec + wait)
- popen, pclose (fork + exec + pipe)

Implementation Notes:
- Most process syscalls are RawSyscall
- fork requires careful membrane state handling
- exec replaces address space (membrane cleanup)
- system/popen need shell invocation

TSM Considerations:
- fork: child inherits membrane state (generation counters)
- exec: membrane cleanup before exec
- Credential operations are security-sensitive

Testing:
- Unit: ID operations
- Integration: fork + exec + wait sequences
- E2E: shell command execution via system()
- Security: credential change validation

Success Criteria:
- All unistd.h process functions classified
- fork/exec sequences work correctly
- Credential operations work

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist

- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.

- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:08.631707430Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T21:55:04.018488102Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T21:55:04.018410356Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","process"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.8","depends_on_id":"bd-25n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":899,"issue_id":"bd-2vv.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Process family expansion complete: added 16 new ABI functions. Version script and support matrix updated (307→323 total). All quality gates pass.","created_at":"2026-02-17T21:54:59Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vv.9","title":"Full symbol-universe normalization + support classification pipeline","description":"Background:
- Closing symbol gaps requires a canonical source for the full target symbol universe and support taxonomy.

Scope:
- Import and normalize full target symbol universe, then classify every symbol with current support state and confidence.
- Mark unknown/unverified symbols explicitly.

Deliverables:
1) Normalized symbol universe dataset.
2) Classification pipeline into support matrix.
3) Unknown/unverified symbol action list.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Every target symbol has a non-ambiguous classification state.
- Classification output is reproducible and diff-friendly.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for symbol normalization/classification rules.
- E2E rebuild of support matrix from source data.

-{"id":"bd-2vvx3","title":"PORT: 10 _nss_files_* tail entries (init + initgroups_dyn + 8 parse_*)","description":"Close the rest of the _nss_files_* parity surface — 10 entries. (1) _nss_files_init() — module initialization no-op (returns void; some glibc versions return enum nss_status, we return NSS_STATUS_SUCCESS). (2) _nss_files_initgroups_dyn(user, gid, *start, *size, **groupsp, limit, *errnop) — supplementary group lookup. Stub returns NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND and sets *errnop = ENOENT when non-NULL; output slots untouched. (3-10) Eight parse_* helpers — _nss_files_parse_etherent / parse_grent / parse_netent / parse_protoent / parse_pwent / parse_rpcent / parse_servent / parse_sgent. Each takes (char *line, struct *result, struct parser_data *data, size_t buflen, int *errnop) and returns int: 1 = parsed, 0 = malformed (caller skips), -1 = ERANGE.  {"id":"bd-2wp","title":"Perf baseline suite: hyperfine + criterion for strict/hardened critical paths","description":"Extreme optimization mapping.

Deliverables:
- Define canonical benchmark commands for allocator, string hot paths, and membrane decision path.
- Capture p50/p95/p99 baseline snapshots per mode.

Acceptance:
- Baselines regenerate deterministically in CI-capable environment.
- Regressions are detectable with clear thresholds.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): perf_baseline_test (8 pass) + gate script. Enforced suites covered, regression thresholds match budget policy, regeneration procedure complete.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:11.789015856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z","close_reason":"Perf baseline suite operational. 8 harness tests pass. check_perf_baseline.sh validates baseline coverage, benchmark suites, percentile targets, and regression thresholds.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline","critique","perf"]}
 {"id":"bd-2x5","title":"Epic: Generational Arena & Allocator Subsystem","description":"Generational arena + quarantine allocator: UAF detection with P=1, per-allocation generation counter, freed blocks held in quarantine, 16-byte SipHash fingerprint + 8-byte canary per allocation. Size classes + thread cache. Covers malloc/calloc/realloc/free family and all allocation-related symbols.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"SnowyCat","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:58:21.541489763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T19:27:38.658440970Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T19:27:38.658419059Z","close_reason":"Allocator E2E strict+hardened parity now passes; allocator core unit-test slice green; prior termination blocker no longer reproduces","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocator","arena","epic","frankenlibc","memory"],"comments":[{"id":900,"issue_id":"bd-2x5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:03Z"},{"id":901,"issue_id":"bd-2x5","author":"Codex","text":"Starting execution on bd-2x5 based on bv robot triage (top actionable P0). Immediate objective: unblock allocator E2E closure by resolving the global LD_PRELOAD startup/interposition hang noted in bd-2x5.5. I will drive a reproduce -> isolate -> patch -> verify loop with deterministic logs and explicit strict+hardened evidence.","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:27:24Z"},{"id":902,"issue_id":"bd-2x5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution update (2026-02-14): I completed a deep reproduce/isolate pass on the LD_PRELOAD allocator E2E timeout path with multiple strict/hardened trace sets. Current state: /bin/true now exits 0 in strict+hardened, allocator fixtures run and print PASS text in some runs, but processes still time out (exit 124) due recursive runtime-kernel/panic-hook interactions during interposed libc paths (observed around puts/strlen/write/posix_memalign and runtime_math observe/lock paths). I implemented guarded mitigations in runtime_policy + allocator/string guards and added runtime_policy unit tests; blocker remains unresolved for bd-2x5.5 and I attached next-step boundary in bd-2x5.5 comment.","created_at":"2026-02-14T05:22:40Z"},{"id":903,"issue_id":"bd-2x5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Latest isolate update (2026-02-14): allocator fixture logic now executes to PASS output under strict+hardened, but process-termination path remains stuck (timeout 124) with repeated runtime-kernel panic fallback messages. This narrows the epic blocker to shutdown/stdio interposition recursion rather than core allocation correctness; bd-2x5.5 remains blocked pending termination-path fail-closed fix.","created_at":"2026-02-14T05:23:05Z"},{"id":904,"issue_id":"bd-2x5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closure evidence refresh for bd-2x5 (2026-02-19): prior LD_PRELOAD termination blocker from 2026-02-14 no longer reproduces on current head.

Validation commands and outcomes:

1) Allocator E2E fixture parity vs host (strict + hardened)
- Command: `TIMEOUT_SECONDS=20 rch exec -- bash scripts/check_allocator_e2e.sh`
- Result: PASS
  - `[PASS] fixture_malloc host=0 strict=0 hardened=0 strict_match=True hardened_match=True`
  - `[PASS] fixture_malloc_stress host=0 strict=0 hardened=0 strict_match=True hardened_match=True`
- Artifacts:
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/report.json`
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/fixture_malloc/host/stdout.txt`
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/fixture_malloc/strict/stdout.txt`
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/fixture_malloc/hardened/stdout.txt`
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/fixture_malloc_stress/host/stdout.txt`

  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/fixture_malloc_stress/hardened/stdout.txt`

2) Allocator core unit-test slice (arena/quarantine/cache invariants)
- Command: `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core malloc::allocator -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`
- Result: PASS
  - 23 allocator tests passed, 0 failed
  - Includes lifecycle/invariant tests and realloc/calloc/thread-cache scenarios.

This satisfies the epic’s outstanding closure blocker and provides deterministic strict+hardened evidence artifacts.

@@ -9059,6 +9059,166 @@ nss_files_parse_stub!(_nss_files_parse_rpcent);
 nss_files_parse_stub!(_nss_files_parse_servent);
 nss_files_parse_stub!(_nss_files_parse_sgent);
 
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// _nss_dns_* NSS DNS plugin lookup stubs
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+//
+// Each returns NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND (= 0) and sets *errnop = ENOENT
+// + *h_errnop = HOST_NOT_FOUND (= 1) when non-NULL, so the NSS
+// dispatch layer falls through to the next module. Without a real
+// resolver-backed NSS_DNS module we never claim to have an answer.
+
+#[inline]
+unsafe fn nss_set_h_errnop_host_not_found(h_errnop: *mut c_int) {
+    if !h_errnop.is_null() {
+        // SAFETY: caller-supplied writable slot per NSS contract.
+        unsafe { *h_errnop = 1 }; // HOST_NOT_FOUND
+    }
+}
+
+/// `_nss_dns_getcanonname_r(name, *buffer, buflen, **result,
+/// *errnop, *h_errnop) -> nss_status` — return canonical name for
+/// `name`. Stub: NOTFOUND + h_errno = HOST_NOT_FOUND.
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn _nss_dns_getcanonname_r(
+    _name: *const c_char,
+    _buffer: *mut c_char,
+    _buflen: usize,
+    _result: *mut *mut c_char,
+    errnop: *mut c_int,
+    h_errnop: *mut c_int,
+) -> c_int {
+    unsafe { nss_set_errnop_enoent(errnop) };
+    unsafe { nss_set_h_errnop_host_not_found(h_errnop) };
+    NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND
+}
+
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn _nss_dns_gethostbyname_r(
+    _name: *const c_char,
+    _result: *mut c_void,
+    _buffer: *mut c_char,
+    _buflen: usize,
+    errnop: *mut c_int,
+    h_errnop: *mut c_int,
+) -> c_int {
+    unsafe { nss_set_errnop_enoent(errnop) };
+    unsafe { nss_set_h_errnop_host_not_found(h_errnop) };
+    NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND
+}
+
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn _nss_dns_gethostbyname2_r(
+    _name: *const c_char,
+    _af: c_int,
+    _result: *mut c_void,
+    _buffer: *mut c_char,
+    _buflen: usize,
+    errnop: *mut c_int,
+    h_errnop: *mut c_int,
+) -> c_int {
+    unsafe { nss_set_errnop_enoent(errnop) };
+    unsafe { nss_set_h_errnop_host_not_found(h_errnop) };
+    NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND
+}
+
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn _nss_dns_gethostbyname3_r(
+    _name: *const c_char,
+    _af: c_int,
+    _result: *mut c_void,
+    _buffer: *mut c_char,
+    _buflen: usize,
+    errnop: *mut c_int,
+    h_errnop: *mut c_int,
+    _ttlp: *mut i32,
+    _canonp: *mut *mut c_char,
+) -> c_int {
+    unsafe { nss_set_errnop_enoent(errnop) };
+    unsafe { nss_set_h_errnop_host_not_found(h_errnop) };
+    NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND
+}
+
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn _nss_dns_gethostbyname4_r(
+    _name: *const c_char,
+    _pat: *mut *mut c_void,
+    _buffer: *mut c_char,
+    _buflen: usize,
+    errnop: *mut c_int,
+    h_errnop: *mut c_int,
+    _ttlp: *mut i32,
+) -> c_int {
+    unsafe { nss_set_errnop_enoent(errnop) };
+    unsafe { nss_set_h_errnop_host_not_found(h_errnop) };
+    NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND
+}
+
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn _nss_dns_gethostbyaddr_r(
+    _addr: *const c_void,
+    _len: usize,
+    _af: c_int,

+}
+
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]

+    _result: *mut c_void,

+    unsafe { nss_set_h_errnop_host_not_found(h_errnop) };
+    NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND
+}
+
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn _nss_dns_getnetbyname_r(
+    _name: *const c_char,
+    _result: *mut c_void,
+    _buffer: *mut c_char,
+    _buflen: usize,
+    errnop: *mut c_int,
+    h_errnop: *mut c_int,
+) -> c_int {
+    unsafe { nss_set_errnop_enoent(errnop) };
+    unsafe { nss_set_h_errnop_host_not_found(h_errnop) };
+    NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND
+}
+
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn _nss_dns_getnetbyaddr_r(
+    _net: u32,
+    _af: c_int,
+    _result: *mut c_void,
+    _buffer: *mut c_char,
+    _buflen: usize,
+    errnop: *mut c_int,
+    h_errnop: *mut c_int,
+) -> c_int {
+    unsafe { nss_set_errnop_enoent(errnop) };
+    unsafe { nss_set_h_errnop_host_not_found(h_errnop) };
+    NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND
+}
+
 // CRYPT_B64 / crypt_b64_encode / crypt_sha512 / crypt_sha256 / crypt_md5
